channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-2117] sanityn test 18 lov_attr_get_raid0()) ASSERTION( atomic_read(&lsm->lsm_refc) > 1 )
item.description:
Running sanityn I hit this assertion today.
I have a kernel crashdump if more info is needed file

[ 4848.384105] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanityn test 18: mmap sanity check =================================== 00:56:56 (1349758616)
[ 4848.455414] LustreError: 380:0:(lov_object.c:392:lov_attr_get_raid0()) ASSERTION( atomic_read(&lsm->lsm_refc) > 1 ) failed: 
[ 4848.456184] LustreError: 380:0:(lov_object.c:392:lov_attr_get_raid0()) LBUG
[ 4848.456559] Pid: 380, comm: mmap_sanity
[ 4848.456863] 
[ 4848.456864] Call Trace:
[ 4848.457463]  [<ffffffffa0b34915>] libcfs_debug_dumpstack+0x55/0x80 [libcfs]
[ 4848.457854]  [<ffffffffa0b34f27>] lbug_with_loc+0x47/0xb0 [libcfs]
[ 4848.458219]  [<ffffffffa059008d>] lov_attr_get_raid0+0x2fd/0x340 [lov]
[ 4848.458606]  [<ffffffffa058debe>] lov_attr_get+0x1e/0x60 [lov]
[ 4848.459007]  [<ffffffffa0cabb48>] cl_object_attr_get+0x78/0x180 [obdclass]
[ 4848.459400]  [<ffffffffa0ab7636>] ccc_lock_state+0x196/0x3b0 [lustre]
[ 4848.459786]  [<ffffffffa0cb2da8>] cl_lock_state_signal+0x68/0x160 [obdclass]
[ 4848.460114]  [<ffffffffa0cb2f24>] cl_lock_state_set+0x84/0x1d0 [obdclass]
[ 4848.460514]  [<ffffffffa0cb3c82>] cl_wait_try+0x122/0x310 [obdclass]
[ 4848.460890]  [<ffffffffa0cb7d95>] cl_wait+0x65/0x270 [obdclass]
[ 4848.461301]  [<ffffffffa0cbe388>] cl_io_lock+0x498/0x5b0 [obdclass]
[ 4848.461694]  [<ffffffffa0cbe542>] cl_io_loop+0xa2/0x1b0 [obdclass]
[ 4848.461994]  [<ffffffffa0a6c22b>] ll_file_io_generic+0x42b/0x550 [lustre]
[ 4848.462377]  [<ffffffffa0a6d11c>] ll_file_aio_write+0x13c/0x2c0 [lustre]
[ 4848.462715]  [<ffffffffa0a6d409>] ll_file_write+0x169/0x2a0 [lustre]
[ 4848.463053]  [<ffffffff8117b2e8>] vfs_write+0xb8/0x1a0
[ 4848.463057]  [<ffffffff810d5192>] ? audit_syscall_entry+0x272/0x2a0
[ 4848.463060]  [<ffffffff8117bbb1>] sys_write+0x51/0x90
[ 4848.463064]  [<ffffffff8100b0f2>] system_call_fastpath+0x16/0x1b
[ 4848.463068] 
[ 4848.463552] Kernel panic - not syncing: LBUG
[ 4848.463554] Pid: 380, comm: mmap_sanity Not tainted 2.6.32-debug #6
[ 4848.463555] Call Trace:
[ 4848.463559]  [<ffffffff814f75e4>] ? panic+0xa0/0x168
[ 4848.463569]  [<ffffffffa0b34f7b>] ? lbug_with_loc+0x9b/0xb0 [libcfs]
[ 4848.463582]  [<ffffffffa059008d>] ? lov_attr_get_raid0+0x2fd/0x340 [lov]
[ 4848.463590]  [<ffffffffa058debe>] ? lov_attr_get+0x1e/0x60 [lov]
[ 4848.463611]  [<ffffffffa0cabb48>] ? cl_object_attr_get+0x78/0x180 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463625]  [<ffffffffa0ab7636>] ? ccc_lock_state+0x196/0x3b0 [lustre]
[ 4848.463643]  [<ffffffffa0cb2da8>] ? cl_lock_state_signal+0x68/0x160 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463660]  [<ffffffffa0cb2f24>] ? cl_lock_state_set+0x84/0x1d0 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463677]  [<ffffffffa0cb3c82>] ? cl_wait_try+0x122/0x310 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463694]  [<ffffffffa0cb7d95>] ? cl_wait+0x65/0x270 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463711]  [<ffffffffa0cbe388>] ? cl_io_lock+0x498/0x5b0 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463727]  [<ffffffffa0cbe542>] ? cl_io_loop+0xa2/0x1b0 [obdclass]
[ 4848.463736]  [<ffffffffa0a6c22b>] ? ll_file_io_generic+0x42b/0x550 [lustre]
[ 4848.463746]  [<ffffffffa0a6d11c>] ? ll_file_aio_write+0x13c/0x2c0 [lustre]
[ 4848.463755]  [<ffffffffa0a6d409>] ? ll_file_write+0x169/0x2a0 [lustre]
[ 4848.463757]  [<ffffffff8117b2e8>] ? vfs_write+0xb8/0x1a0
[ 4848.463759]  [<ffffffff810d5192>] ? audit_syscall_entry+0x272/0x2a0
[ 4848.463760]  [<ffffffff8117bbb1>] ? sys_write+0x51/0x90
[ 4848.463763]  [<ffffffff8100b0f2>] ? system_call_fastpath+0x16/0x1b

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: This issue was imported by the layout lock patch on the client side, so it will be fixed in LU-1876(patch http://review.whamcloud.com/4416)

The root cause of this problem is that when a cl_lock belonging to other object is requested(this happens for mmap case), the stripe's refcount may not be greater than 1 if there is no pending IO for it.

Probably this assertion is too restricted here. Anyway, the layout can't go away if there exists locks. So I think we can fix this by removing this assertion.
Had this happen again after 22 hours of running sanityn.sh in a loop
